name: Lai Ching-te
title: President of the Republic of China (Taiwan)

compatibilityOverrides.reason: Beijing views DPP leadership as separatist; existential cross-strait tension
De facto security guarantor under Taiwan Relations Act
Shared democratic values and regional security concerns about China

biography: Lai Ching-te, a physician turned politician, won Taiwan's presidency as the Democratic Progressive Party candidate, representing continuity in the island's assertion of de facto independence. His INFJ idealism drives a principled stance on Taiwanese identity and democratic values, even as cross-strait military pressure from China intensifies. He leads the world's most critical semiconductor supply chain while navigating a minority legislature and the ever-present threat of Chinese coercion, making Taiwan the most dangerous flashpoint in the Indo-Pacific.
